articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the only highlight was the UK GDP report. The data showed a record fourth straight month of increase in services output. articles.title: Oil: Ceasefire hopes keep prices steady – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team notes that Oil prices held around USD 95 per barrel as markets focused on prospects for a ceasefire between the US and Iran and a potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: UK GDP Beats Expectations at 0.5% mom with Broad-Based February Growth
articles.expand: UK economic activity strengthened in February, with GDP rising 0.5% mom, well above expectations of 0.1% mom. The expansion was broad-based, with both services and production increasing by 0.5%, while construction posted a stronger 1.0% gain, pointing to a solid rebound in output. On a three-month basis, growth also improved. GDP expanded by 0.5% i...

articles.expand: The UK Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew 0.5% MoM in February, following a 0% reported in January, the latest data published by the Office for National Statistics (ONS) showed on Thursday.

articles.title: VAT Group Q1 Sales Down 20%; Reiterates 2026 Guidance
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - VAT Group AG (19V.F, VACN.SW) reported first quarter sales of 221 million Swiss francs, down 20% year on year, or down 9% on a constant currency basis. Order intake was 356 million francs, up 47% year on year, or was up 67% on a constant currency basis.
